description:
OL 316 (REV. 7/2020) WWW
Autonomous Mode 
Conventional Mode
Additional information attached.
On March 3, 2024 at 3:12 PM PT a Waymo Autonomous Vehicle ("Waymo AV") operating in San Francisco, California was in a collision 
involving a pick-up truck near the 2300 block of Cesar Chavez Street. 
 
The Waymo AV was traveling westbound on Cesar Chavez Street towards 26th Street when it approached a pick-up truck which was 
oriented with its rear partially in the roadway on the north side of Cesar Chavez street. The pick-up truck was stationary but its reverse lights 
were illuminated. The Waymo AV came to a stop momentarily to yield to the pick-up truck, then began moving again to pass the pick-up 
truck, which had remained stopped. After the Waymo AV started moving, the pick-up truck began reversing further out of the driveway and 
the rear of the pick-up truck made contact with the right side of the Waymo AV. At the time of the impact, the Waymo AV's Level 4 ADS 
was engaged in autonomous mode, and a test driver was present (in the driver’s seating position). Both vehicles sustained damage.
Print
Clear Form
